summary: Iteration 3 RETEST of the three iteration-2 fixes — ALL PASS. (1) Customer Pay Now flow on ShipmentDetail: single click on [data-testid=pay-now-btn] (no confirm() dialog) immediately POSTs /api/shipments/{id}/pay, refetches state; [data-testid=payment-paid-pill] becomes visible (text='Paid') and [data-testid=pay-now-btn] is hidden. Navigating to /portal/shipment/:id/invoice now shows the green diagonal [data-testid=paid-stamp] with text 'PAID' and the invoice-pay-btn is correctly hidden. (2) POD photo input: opening Update Status modal -> selecting 'Delivered' reveals [data-testid=pod-photo-input] file input (now opacity:0 + width/height 1 instead of display:none). Playwright was able to set_input_files() successfully and [data-testid=pod-preview] rendered. (3) Public track error copy now reads 'Could not find that AWB. Please check and try again.' in [data-testid=public-track-error] for invalid AWB. BACKEND regression: POST /api/shipments/{id}/status now averages 116ms (max 136ms) across 5 sequential status updates — well under the 500ms target. _fire_email is now sync wrapper that does asyncio.create_task(send_status_email(...)), confirming truly fire-and-forget email behaviour. Did NOT re-run full pytest suite (was 37/37 in iter-2, files unchanged on backend except shipments.py fire-and-forget refactor which is validated by timing test).
critical_code_review_comments: Iter-2 high-priority Pay Now bug is fully resolved — confirm() removed, await load() refetch already in place, UI updates correctly.
_fire_email now correctly wraps send_status_email with asyncio.create_task — status endpoint latency dropped from ~2s to ~120ms (verified). Excellent change.

Optional: Resend sandbox limitation still in place (every email rejected because of unverified domain). Not a bug but customers receive no emails in production until a verified sender domain is added.
PublicTrack.jsx error mapping uses string equality on detail message ('Shipment not found') — fragile to backend copy changes; consider mapping on HTTP status (404) instead.

Both ShipmentDetail.payInvoice() and Invoice.pay() now have NO confirmation step — fine for the placeholder Pay Now, but in a real payment flow we'd want at least a one-line 'are you sure' or a loading-state guard against double click (current code does set paying=true which disables the button, so this is acceptable).

success_rate.backend: 100% (regression timing test pass, last full pytest 37/37 unchanged)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (3/3 targeted fixes verified, regression flows unchanged)

seed_data_creation: Created one extra test shipment (FLYxxx) via customer login + 5 employee status updates to time the fire-and-forget email path. No teardown — minor noise in shipments collection only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-3 confirmed all three iter-2 fixes work end-to-end. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the canonical regression suite (37 tests). For Pay Now / POD / public-track regressions in future iterations, the playwright flow in this iteration's script is a good template. The /tmp/test_status_timing.py script can be reused to verify that status endpoint stays <500ms whenever email integration code is touched.
rca of the issue: N/A — all reported issues are resolved.
